title: Pickover Biomorphs
purpose: Shader context repository implementing Clifford Pickover's biomorph algorithm as a full three-pass rendering pipeline. Eight taxonomically-named species rendered as microscopy specimens — darkfield, phase contrast, brightfield, and fluorescence. Primary inputs for RepoScripter: complex iteration aesthetics, biological stain palettes, orbit-derived anatomy coloring, and microscopy optical simulation.
visual_motifs: darkfield glow — bright specimen on absolute black
birefringent glass substrate with Newton rings
three-channel fluorescence composite (GFP green / RFP red / DAPI blue)
orbit boundary tendrils at high-wander regions
nested convergence zones as anatomical depth
techniques: axis-aligned escape condition: |Re(z)| > R AND |Im(z)| > R (Pickover's biomorph modification)
wander metric: continuous orbit activity integral Σ||zₙ|| − ||zₙ₋₁||
three independent coloring axes: iter_ratio, wander, final_arg
GLSL complex arithmetic: cmul, cpow, csin, ccos, cexp, clog
Julia iteration (fixed c, varying z₀) for 7 species
Mandelbrot iteration (fixed z₀=0, varying c) for _spore
biological stain palette mapping (Gram, H&E, Giemsa, Crystal Violet, Periodic Acid-Schiff)
luminance-gradient edge detection for phase-contrast halo
three-channel false-color fluorescence composite with bleed-through and photobleaching
Köhler illumination simulation with depth-of-field softening
Brownian scatter and thermal caustics for saline medium
film grain via per-pixel hash function
palette: darkfield: near-black background (#050508), specimen glow in amber/gold or cyan/white
phase contrast: grey background, bright white halos, interior shadow
brightfield (Giemsa): azure blue / magenta / pale pink stain
brightfield (Crystal Violet): deep violet on pale substrate
fluorescence: GFP green (#39ff6a), RFP orange-red (#ff4422), DAPI blue (#4488ff) on black
insectoid: amber-gold chitin, cyan haemolymph, dark background
embryonic: pale membrane gold, nucleus pink-violet, yolk warm amber
radiolarian: silicate silver-white spines, aqua interior, darkfield black
spirochete: safranin red (gram-negative), dark olive membrane, phase-grey background
diatom: crystal violet / pale yellow striae, brightfield warm white
ctenophore: iridescent teal-to-magenta banding, deep blue-black ocean
spore: warm amber outer coat, pale grey interior, brightfield
motion: mouse x/y maps to Re(c)/Im(c) — lateral mouse motion mutates species morphology in real time
click freezes organism at current c value
u_time drives membrane pulsing and embryonic oscillation
environmental shaders use u_time for Brownian thermal drift and caustic animation
no camera motion — viewport is fixed; zoom is via coordinate scaling only
geometry: complex plane iteration — each pixel is a starting point z₀
axis-aligned symmetry created by Re/Im escape condition
radial symmetry in species using sin(z), cos(z), exp(z)
bilateral symmetry in diatom (cosine) and insectoid (power) species
nested basin boundaries create layered anatomical depth
orbit trap geometry: wander accumulator traces filament paths
avoid: pure SDF geometric modeling with no orbital or biological context
clean modernist abstract geometry (no organic chaos)
standard Mandelbrot/Julia sets without axis-aligned escape modification
physically-based rendering pipelines (PBR) — this is optical simulation, not material rendering
